prompt: A candid, photorealistic street documentary photo of three primary school children buying snacks at a small old-fashioned neighborhood convenience shop in China, shot in soft natural daylight. The scene is framed straight-on at storefront level, with a slightly compressed everyday snapshot feel, realistic textures, and subtle depth. On the left, one schoolgirl stands in profile wearing a light pink zip-up jacket, dark jeans, and a red scarf, her black hair tied in a ponytail with a white headband, looking down at coins or a small item in her hands. In the center, one schoolboy stands with his back to the camera in a bright blue school tracksuit and a navy-and-red backpack featuring a Mickey Mouse graphic, leaning slightly toward the counter. On the right, one younger schoolgirl with two low pigtails tied with pale pink hair ties leans against a large chest freezer, wearing a white long-sleeve top and blue athletic pants, with a pink backpack resting beside her. Behind the counter, inside the dim shop interior, one shopkeeper in a pink top with a cartoon character print stands partially obscured in shadow. The counter is cluttered with packaged snacks, clear plastic bags of candy, colorful biscuit boxes, jars, and small goods; the shelves behind are densely packed with drinks and snacks. A bunch of multicolored balloons hangs near the upper center of the shop entrance. The chest freezer in the foreground is white with a green lid trim and green Chinese branding on the side. The storefront is worn and modest, with peeling beige walls, a posted calendar or notice sheet on the left wall, metal-framed windows, and an open service window. Include authentic Chinese neighborhood shop details, natural messiness, believable lighting, muted urban colors, and an unposed slice-of-life atmosphere. Faces should be softly obscured or turned away, emphasizing realism and privacy rather than portrait detail.
